description_long: This sculpture depicts a bearded male figure draped in a classical robe, standing upright on a rectangular plinth. The figure is partially nude, with the robe covering his lower body and draped over one shoulder. He holds a staff around which a large serpent is coiled, a motif often associated with healing and medicine. The detailing of the hair, facial features, and folds of the robe are finely rendered, giving the sculpture a lifelike and dignified appearance. The back of the figure is exposed, showing muscular definition and the continuation of the robe. The entire piece appears to be made from a dark, stone-like material, giving it a solid and monumental presence.
description: A classical-style sculpture of a bearded man in a robe holding a staff entwined by a serpent, standing on a rectangular base. The figure is detailed and lifelike, with a dark, stone-like finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bearded man's face, detailed robe, and the serpent coiled around the staff.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the draping of the robe, and the full length of the serpent.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the exposed back, muscular definition, and the robe draped over one shoulder.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side of the head, the robe's folds, and the serpent's body curving around the staff.
attribute_subject_matter: human figure with serpent and staff
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
